title: The Spirituality of RR: Wonder/Awe/Mystery/Sacredness
essence: Transitioning from machinery to phenomenology, Vervaeke argues that the relevance realization apparatus itself, when experienced transparently, constitutes spirituality. Wonder and awe emerge not from external objects but from encountering the mystery of the machinery grounding all knowing—the framing process that can never be framed. Drawing on Costa's 'secular wonder' and Fuller's work on awe as sensibility transcendence, he establishes that spirituality involves participation in and awareness of the primordial processes (religio) that make meaning possible. Mystery here is not theoretical inexplicability but phenomenological unavailability of the framing itself. Sacredness emerges from two opponent poles: Geertz's meta-meaning system protecting against domicide (horror of meaninglessness) and Otto's numinous—the transgressive wonder that takes us to the horizon of intelligibility itself. Together these constitute the experience of sacredness as deep engagement with relevance realization.
keyClaims: Spirituality is fundamentally about experiencing connectedness to religio—the primordial machinery of meaning-making itself.
Wonder and awe are states in which we become perspectivally-participatorily aware of the significance of relevance realization.
Mystery is phenomenologically irreducible: the framing process can never be made a focal object without creating another frame.
Sacredness has two opponent aspects: worldview attunement (meta-assimilation protecting against absurdity) and the numinous (meta-accommodation opening to transgression).
Religio is fundamentally pre-conceptual, pre-propositional, pre-inferential—grounding all subsequent knowing.

concepts.name: religio
concepts.summary: The binding-together of agent and arena through relevance realization; the machinery and experience of connectivity itself.

concepts.name: wonder
concepts.summary: Non-focal, participatory state of opening to the inexhaustibility of reality; being mode.

concepts.name: awe
concepts.summary: Wonder bordering on horror; opening up to mysteries while recognizing limits; combines fascination with humility.

concepts.name: mystery
concepts.summary: Phenomenological irreducibility of the framing process; not theoretical inexplicability but perspectival unavailability.

concepts.name: sacredness
concepts.summary: Experience of the machinery of relevance realization; meta-assimilation (homecoming) and meta-accommodation (transgression).

concepts.name: numinous
concepts.summary: Aspect of sacredness involving fascination and horror; experience of reality's ultimate inadequacy to frames.

concepts.name: theosis
concepts.summary: Transformation into God's likeness; parallels participatory knowing in religio.

concepts.name: anagoge
concepts.summary: Spiritual elevation and transformation; relates to sensibility transcendence in experiencing sacredness.

concepts.name: da'ath
concepts.summary: Hebrew mystical term; intimate experiential knowing akin to participatory knowing of religio.

concepts.name: sati
concepts.summary: Buddhist mindfulness; remembering being-mode as opposed to having-mode cognition.

people.name: Paolo Costa
people.summary: On 'secular wonder' and the atmospheric bubble of significance that grounds meaning.

people.name: Robert Fuller
people.summary: On wonder as emotion-to-spirituality transition; being mode vs. having mode curiosity.

people.name: Clifford Geertz
people.summary: On religion's meta-meaning function protecting against domicide; meta-assimilation aspect of sacredness.

people.name: Rudolf Otto
people.summary: On the numinous as core of holiness; fascination and horror as polarities of sacredness.

people.name: Gabriel Marcel
people.summary: On mystery and sensibility transcendence; transframing as trajectory of opening.

people.name: William James
people.summary: On I-me distinction; the 'I' can never be captured as focal object, only known subsidiarily.

people.name: Heidegger
people.summary: On primordiality of being-in-the-world and foundational caring; influences understanding of religio.

people.name: Huston Smith
people.summary: Perennial philosophy perspective on shared spiritual themes across traditions.

crossRefs.context: Previous episode on consciousness and salience landscaping; this explores its experiential spiritual dimensions.
Next episode develops numinous and horror, symbol, and sacredness further.
Earlier episode on existentialism; Heideggerian themes developed here.

quotableMoments.quote: I'm always aware through my 'I' of my 'me'. I'm always aware through my framing of my framed. I cannot focalise it. I can not make it a focal object. The machinery of Relevance Realization is deeply phenomenologically mysterious.
quotableMoments.context: On the irreducibility of the framing process and mystery at the heart of knowing.

quotableMoments.quote: Wonder is about remembering Sati, your Being, by putting you deeply in touch with Religio.
quotableMoments.context: Connection between wonder, being-mode, and religio.

quotableMoments.quote: There is a deep, non-logical identity, a deep symbolic resonance between the inexhaustibleness of Reality and the inexhaustibleness of Relevance Realization.
quotableMoments.context: On the sacred as inexhaustible coupling of reality and meaning-making.
